rationale: Makes the HiFi-GAN ONNX mel contract explicit in the Rust listenbury project. src/vocoder/input.rs introduces new types: MelConfig {sample_rate_hz, hop_samples, n_fft, win_length, n_mels, f_min_hz, f_max_hz, scale}, MelScale enum (LinearEnergy / NaturalLogEnergy / Log10Energy / DynamicRangeCompressed / ModelSpecific(String)), MelSpectrogram {config, frames: Vec<MelFrame>}, and MelTensorLayout enum (FramesBins / BinsFrames / BatchFramesBins / BatchBinsFrames). VocoderInput::Mel and VocoderInput::MelF0 are changed from &'a [MelFrame] to &'a MelSpectrogram so the config travels with the frames. src/vocoder/hifigan.rs replaces the hardcoded MODEL_MEL_BINS=80 + ad-hoc normalize_mel_for_onnx resample with: HifiganBackend.onnx_config: Option<HifiganOnnxConfig>, expected_mel_config(), and a new load_hifigan_model_config() that reads <model>.config.json (serde_json::Value walk for sampling_rate/hop_length/n_fft/win_length/num_mel_bins/mel_scale/input_layout/input_name/output_name). New validate_mel_compatibility() fails loudly when sample_rate_hz, hop_samples, scale, or n_mels mismatch (with model-path-prefixed error messages). New mel_values_for_layout() routes mel data into [T,M] / [M,T] / [1,T,M] / [1,M,T] tensor shapes per detected/configured layout, with detect_layout_from_shape() pattern-matching shape against n_mels. resolve_hifigan_input/output_name prefer the configured name then fall back to candidate lists. Four new tests cover all four tensor layouts, mismatch validation, and shape detection. src/cli/piper.rs wires synthesize_hifigan_for_say to call backend.expected_mel_config() and pass it into hifigan_mel_f0_from_riper_phonemes_with_config so the mel bin count matches the loaded model. The PR was closed without merge but the diff is a substantive, well-tested contract refactor that delivers exactly what the title promises.
